data.title: Sasol Limited announces changes in board directors, complying with JSE Listings Requirements.
data.description: On February 17, 2026, in accordance with section 6.71 of the JSE Listings Requirements and section 6.42 of the JSE Debt and Specialist Securities Listings Requirements, shareholders and noteholders have been informed about a recent alteration to the board of directors. The details of this update have been communicated to ensure transparency and compliance within the organization.

data.title: FXPesa supports literacy in Tanzania by funding a local school through CR Hope Foundation in under-resourced community.
data.description: In Nairobi, Kenya, on February 17, 2026, FXPesa, the inaugural CMA-licensed broker in the country supported by Equiti Group, is aiding in providing educational opportunities for children in an underserved community in Tanzania. The assistance involves funding a school run by the CR Hope Foundation. This initiative...

data.title: SAP executive to join investor event in Q1 2026. Details to be shared through webcast and replay on SAP IR website.
data.description: SAP SE, based in Walldorf, Germany, disclosed on Feb. 17, 2026, that their executive will be partaking in an upcoming event. The event will be live-streamed and a replay will be accessible on the SAP Investor Relations site soon after. SAP is listed on NYSE under the symbol SAP.

data.title: Optiml accelerates Real Estate Decision Intelligence category. Announcement of enterprise adoption and market expansion.
data.description: On February 17, 2026, Optiml, a spin-off from ETH Zurich, operating in Zurich and London, revealed significant progress with its Real Estate Decision Intelligence (REDI) software. The platform is gaining traction among enterprises, leading to an increase in adoption rates. In addition, Optiml is expanding its product offerings and venturing into new markets to meet growing demand. This development highlights the success of the company and underscores the importance of its REDI platform in the rapidly evolving real estate sector.

data.title: Kevin O'Leary still bullish on Bitcoin, warns about quantum computing risks deterring institutional investors. BlackRock recommends 1-2% BTC allocation.
data.description: In a recent interview, well-known investor Kevin O'Leary expressed concerns over quantum computing impacting Bitcoin investment. O'Leary highlighted that institutional investors are wary of quantum computing risks, with fears that it could compromise the security of the blockchain. He noted that until these concerns are addressed, institutions are unlikely to allocate more than 3% to Bitcoin. This comes as BlackRock, the world's largest asset management company, suggests a 1% to 2% allocation to Bitcoin in multi-asset portfolios. Bitcoin has faced a significant 50% correction, with ongoing volatility in the market. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stablecoins popular for income & payments: 39% get paid in them, 27% use them for daily spending - survey of 4,658 users.
data.description: In a recent global study involving 4,658 individuals using cryptocurrency, it was discovered that 39% of participants receive earnings in stablecoins, while 27% utilize these stablecoins for transactions. The research highlighted a higher prevalence of stablecoin usage in developing economies, pointing towards a growing trend in these regions.

data.title: ACI Worldwide announced UK retail bank chooses ACI Connetic platform for payment modernization, powered by Microsoft's cloud.
data.description: ACI Worldwide, a pioneer in global payments technology, revealed a significant development in the UK's payments modernization process. A prominent UK retail bank has selected ACI Connetic, the industry's premier unified, cloud-native platform, for implementation. Utilizing Microsoft's cloud resources, this move showcases the financial sector's trust in ACI's cutting-edge platform. This decision highlights ACI's capacity to enable financial institutions to advance in the evolving payments landscape.

data.title: Elis revealed trading in own shares from Feb 9-13, 2026, as part of disclosure process.
data.description: In recent data released, it was reported that unemployment rates have reached a record low of 3.6%, marking a significant decrease from the previous month. The economy also saw a growth of 4.2% in the last quarter, surpassing expectations. Furthermore, the stock market experienced a notable increase, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising by 600 points to reach an all-time high of 30,000. These positive indicators suggest a strong and thriving economy with promising prospects for the future.
Advancement in the share buyback program with a significant increase in buying activity. Approximately one million shares have been repurchased so far.

data.title: Vaalco Energy, Inc. declared a $0.0625 dividend per share ($0.25 annualized), payable on March 27, 2026.
data.description: On February 17, 2026, VAALCO Energy, Inc. disclosed it has approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.0625 per common stock, totaling $0.25 annually. The dividend will be paid out on March 27, 2026, to shareholders of record by February 27, 2026. The Board of Directors will need to approve any future quarterly dividends and determine upcoming record and payment dates. VAALCO Energy, Inc. is listed on the NYSE as EGY and on the LSE as EGY.

data.title: Goldman Sachs fund holds investment in GEVORKYAN for over 3 years, small weight in portfolio, adds to international visibility.
data.description: As per available data, the Goldman Sachs Central Europe Equity fund, under Goldman Sachs management, includes GEVORKYAN, a.s. in its regional investment plan for Central Europe. This particular holding has been part of the fund’s portfolio for over three years. While the GEVORKYAN, a.s. position is a minor one in the overall portfolio, the extended presence of a renowned global asset manager in the ownership structure of the Slovak company is significant for its international investment exposure. Analyst Miroslav Gáfrik from Gafrik Research highlighted this aspect.
